Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Apr-27-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Christian Einfeld, Esq. appeared for petitioner Raul Velasco, the Guardian ad Litem for the minor, Ricardo Velasco
Both the Guardian and the minor's appearance were waived at the last calling of the case.
Carl E. Kadlic, Esq. appeared for defendants Fujita Masayoshi and Fuji Gardens, Inc.

On the basis of evidence received, and good cause appearing, the Petition is granted and compromise is approved.

Settlement Amount - $151,970.69.
Attorney Fees - $46,127.43.
Medical Expenses - $4,715.12.
Costs - $28,487.72.

The net balance of $72,640.42 shall be used for purchase of an ANNUITY as described in Attachment 7c(2)(b) to the Order Approving the Compromise.

Order Approving the Petition to Approve the Compromise of a Disputed Claim of a Minor was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Hearing On The Petition To Approve The Compromise Of A Disputed Claim Of A Minor.
Off Calendar Apr-15-2010 Continued To Apr-27-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Christian Einfeldt, Esq. appeared with petitioner Raul Velasco, who was present as Guardian ad Litem for the minor Ricardo Velasco, who was also present at today's hearing.
Petitioner and the minor were placed under oath and examined.
Carl E. Kadlic, Esql apeared for defendants.

Good cause appearing, defendants' request for a CONTINUANCE is GRANTED. Matter is continued to April 27, 2010 @ 9:00 a.m. for further hearing on the petition to approve the compromise of a disputed claim of a minor. The Court waives petitioner and the minor's appearance at the next calling of the case.
